cta-systematic-macro.AMZN: Not on shortlist and trend not confirmed in screen; absent a fired long signal the rule holds no position.
cta-systematic-macro.APP: Positive 4w momentum (+18.5%) but trend not cleanly confirmed: ADX 29.6 and price 539 essentially AT SMA200 541. Token position awaiting confirmation.
cta-systematic-macro.BAC: No trend: ADX 16.1, price 52 oscillating around SMA200 52, RSI 62 but no trend strength. No position.
cta-systematic-macro.BMY: Mixed: price 56.9 > SMA200 53.2 but MACD hist -0.08, ADX 15.0 (no trend strength). No confirmed signal -> no position.
cta-systematic-macro.C: Confirmed uptrend: price 128 > EMA20 128 > EMA50 125 > SMA200 111.5, MACD hist +0.90, RSI 63, ADX 21.4. Moderate but clean trend -> follow it.
cta-systematic-macro.CHTR: Deep confirmed downtrend: price 142 << SMA200 217, ADX 38.0, RSI 29.5, MACD deeply negative. Long-only -> out.
cta-systematic-macro.CI: No trend: ADX 13.5, price ~283 hugging EMA20/EMA50/SMA200 all near 282, flat MACD. Rule says no position when no trend.
cta-systematic-macro.CL: Weak: ADX 20.2, MACD hist -0.54, RSI 45. No confirmed advancing trend -> no position.
cta-systematic-macro.CMCSA: Confirmed downtrend: price 24.7 < EMA50 26.3 < SMA200 29.1, ADX 30.3 (strong), RSI 31, MACD hist negative. Long-only -> stay out, no position.
cta-systematic-macro.COP: No trend: ADX 11.3, price 118.6 == EMA20 118.6, MACD negative. Flat -> no signal.
cta-systematic-macro.CVS: Confirmed uptrend: price 91.8 > EMA50 86.9 > SMA200 79.1, ADX 35.1 (strong), RSI 66. MACD hist marginally negative -> sized mid-tier.
cta-systematic-macro.CVX: No trend: ADX 11.8, price 188 hugging EMA50 188.3, MACD slightly negative. Range-bound, no position.
cta-systematic-macro.DELL: Top time-series momentum (+61.9% 4w), ADX 56 (very strong trend), bullish stack price > EMA20 > EMA50 > SMA200, MACD hist positive. Standout established trend -> max-ride.
cta-systematic-macro.DUK: Down-bias: price 123.5 < EMA50 125.2, MACD negative, RSI 43. ADX 26 but pointing lower -> no long signal.
cta-systematic-macro.ELV: Strong confirmed uptrend: EMA20 393 > EMA50 369 > SMA200 338, ADX 49.0 (very strong), RSI 70. MACD hist near-flat -> sized just below top tier.
cta-systematic-macro.FTNT: Strongest trend-strength of the set (ADX 60.8), +24.6% 4w, perfect bullish MA stack, price near upper Bollinger. Persistent breakout -> follow.
cta-systematic-macro.HCA: Strong confirmed downtrend: price 394 < EMA50 427 < SMA200 461, ADX 54.9, RSI 22.7, MACD deeply negative. Long-only -> firmly out.
cta-systematic-macro.HLT: Choppy: stack bullish but ADX 18.3 below trend threshold. Trend not established -> no position (vs MAR which confirmed).
cta-systematic-macro.JPM: Borderline: price 302 < SMA200 306, ADX 20.4, MACD hist +1.41. Not confirmed above 200-day reference -> no position.
cta-systematic-macro.KO: Marginal: stack ok (79>78.5>73) but ADX 22, MACD hist -0.34 rolling, RSI 50. Not a confirmed advancing trend -> no position.
cta-systematic-macro.LLY: Strong confirmed uptrend: price 1064 > EMA50 1014 > SMA200 950, MACD hist +6.58, ADX 33.5, RSI 71. Established trend -> add and ride.
cta-systematic-macro.MAR: Moderate confirmed uptrend: ADX 25.3, +10.9% 4w, clean stack price > EMA20 > EMA50 > SMA200, MACD hist positive. Travel momentum that confirmed.
cta-systematic-macro.MAS: No trend: ADX 12.8, price ~68 flat against EMA20/EMA50/SMA200 all ~68, MACD hist ~0. Range-bound.
cta-systematic-macro.META: Below 200-day: price 611 < SMA200 661, MACD hist -1.71, RSI 40, OBV negative. No long signal.
cta-systematic-macro.MO: Weak: ADX 18.0, MACD hist -0.27 rolling, RSI 53. No confirmed advancing trend -> no position.
cta-systematic-macro.MRK: Stack bullish (116>SMA200 104) but ADX 19.2 below trend threshold; nascent, not confirmed. Rule: wait for confirmation, no position.
cta-systematic-macro.MSFT: Below 200-day: price 422 < SMA200 456, MACD hist -1.89, OBV negative. No long signal.
cta-systematic-macro.NOW: Below 200-day: price 106 < SMA200 139.9 despite positive MACD hist. Recovery not confirmed above reference -> no position.
cta-systematic-macro.NTAP: Confirmed uptrend: ADX 55.8, +45.5% 4w, stack 171 > EMA20 150 > EMA50 129 > SMA200 113, MACD hist positive. Storage trend cluster with DELL/STX.
cta-systematic-macro.NVDA: Decelerating: price 218 > SMA200 188 but MACD hist -2.15, RSI 47, ADX 19.3 below threshold. Trend not currently established -> stay out.
cta-systematic-macro.PFE: No trend: ADX 12.7, price 25.9 oscillating around SMA200 25.85, flat MACD. No directional signal.
cta-systematic-macro.PM: Rolling over: stack ok but MACD hist -1.63, RSI 47, ADX 21.9. Momentum decelerating, not a clean long -> no position.
cta-systematic-macro.STX: Strong trend (ADX 44.3, stack 880 >> SMA200 390) but MACD hist turned negative -- decelerating at top of parabolic move. Sized down per discipline.
cta-systematic-macro.T: Strong downtrend: price 24.3 < EMA50 25.3 < SMA200 26.3, ADX 44.6, RSI 23.9, MACD negative. Firmly out.
cta-systematic-macro.TMUS: Downtrend: price 187 < EMA50 192.5 < SMA200 209.8, RSI 33.9, MACD negative. Stay out.
cta-systematic-macro.UBER: Downtrend: price 72.9 < SMA200 83.0, MACD hist -0.20, ADX 10.2 (no trend strength). Stay out.
cta-systematic-macro.UNH: Confirmed uptrend: EMA20 383 > EMA50 360 > SMA200 329, price ~388, ADX 38.3 (strong persistence), RSI 70. MACD hist marginally negative -> sized just below top tier.
cta-systematic-macro.VZ: Down-bias: price 46.9 below EMA50 47.3, MACD hist -0.30, RSI 35.4. ADX 17.6 weak but no long signal. Stay out.
cta-systematic-macro.WFC: Below 200-day: price 76.7 < SMA200 84.1 despite MACD hist positive. Recovery not above reference -> no long signal.
cta-systematic-macro.XOM: No trend: ADX 11.3, price 152 flat against EMA20/EMA50 ~151.5, MACD hist ~0. Range-bound, no signal.

discretionary-macro.AMZN: Consumer-discretionary plus long-duration cloud beta — wrong on both axes with UMCSENT at 49.8 and rates pinned high. Fade.
discretionary-macro.APP: High-beta, high-duration ad-tech momentum name — the crowded-trade tail the narrow tape and VIX crack threaten most. Not a regime expression; reduce to zero.
discretionary-macro.BAC: More rate/credit-cycle exposed than JPM; the steepener tailwind is offset by recession-tail and AFS-mark risk in a stress crack. Hold at zero.
discretionary-macro.BMY: Same as PFE: pharma without the secular-growth runway; not a regime-expressing name. Hold at zero.
discretionary-macro.C: Lower-quality, higher-beta money-center bank; most exposed to a credit/vol shock. Not my trade in a hostile regime. Hold at zero.
discretionary-macro.CHTR: Highly levered cable into a bear-steepening long end — the worst kind of duration exposure in this regime. Hold, no weight.
discretionary-macro.CI: Health-insurer peer; same defensive bucket as UNH, which is my chosen expression. Hold at zero.
discretionary-macro.CL: Defensive staples with global pricing power and inelastic demand; rate-insensitive cash flow that holds up while the consumer rolls over and the Fed stays sidelined.
discretionary-macro.CMCSA: Media/cable with secular cord-cutting headwinds and consumer-discretionary exposure; not a defensive inflation winner. Hold at zero.
discretionary-macro.COP: Higher-beta domestic E&P leverage to a sustained $90-100 oil regime. If the long end stays bid-down (GS30 5.03) and energy stays the inflation driver, upstream operating leverage outperforms the integrateds. Sized smaller for its higher beta.
discretionary-macro.CVS: Healthcare-services exposure but a messier, idiosyncratic story; not a clean macro-regime expression. No add — single-name minutiae is noise to my top-down lens.
discretionary-macro.CVX: Second integrated major to size the energy overweight as a real-asset inflation hedge plus geopolitical premium. Pays me to hold the macro view while oil sits elevated on the supply shock; diversifies single-name risk inside the primary trade.
discretionary-macro.DELL: AI-infrastructure hardware — same crowded-tech trade I fade, but I express the fade through the mega-caps. Hold at zero; single-name hardware is noise to my top-down view.
discretionary-macro.DUK: Regulated utility with cost pass-through and a bond-proxy profile. Defensive yield ballast that benefits if the VIX crack (21.5) turns into a growth scare that drags the long end back down.
discretionary-macro.ELV: Managed-care peer to UNH; I take the defensive healthcare-insurer view through UNH and hold ELV at zero to keep the sleeve focused.
discretionary-macro.FTNT: Cybersecurity software — long-duration tech I'm fading; not a defensive or inflation name. Hold, no weight.
discretionary-macro.HCA: Hospital operator with real labor-cost inflation exposure on the cost side; not the clean pricing-power pass-through I want defensively. Hold, no weight.
discretionary-macro.HLT: Same discretionary-travel cyclical thesis as MAR; fades in a late-cycle consumer rollover. Hold, no weight.
discretionary-macro.JPM: Bear-steepening helps bank NIM, but in a VIX-crack stress regime with credit-spread and recession-tail risk I don't lead with financials. Best-in-class bank but a hold, not an add this week.
discretionary-macro.KO: Short-duration, inflation-pass-through staples cash flow and a classic late-cycle defensive. With UMCSENT at a series-low 49.8 and tariffs squeezing the consumer, branded-pricing-power staples are where I want defensive weight.
discretionary-macro.LLY: Secular, rate-insensitive earnings compounder whose demand is orthogonal to the consumer-sentiment collapse (UMCSENT 49.8) and tariff cycle. Where I own quality growth while fading crowded AI-tech beta but not exiting growth entirely.
discretionary-macro.MAR: Consumer-discretionary travel cyclical; directly exposed to the consumer-sentiment collapse (UMCSENT 49.8). Wrong side of the regime. Hold at zero.
discretionary-macro.MAS: Building-products name tied to housing and the discretionary consumer; rate-sensitive cyclical, wrong regime. Hold at zero.
discretionary-macro.META: Crowded AI-adjacent mega-cap; Wall Street is calling a pause in the AI trade and the tape is narrow. Long-duration ad-cyclical beta is exactly what I fade in a late-cycle stagflationary tilt.
discretionary-macro.MO: Deep defensive: inelastic-demand tobacco with strong pricing power passes cost-push inflation through and is orthogonal to the consumer-sentiment collapse. Classic late-cycle, low-beta cash-flow sleeve.
discretionary-macro.MRK: On-thesis defensive healthcare cluster name but I express the rate-insensitive pharma view through LLY; MRK is a hold rather than a fresh add this week to avoid over-concentrating the healthcare sleeve.
discretionary-macro.MSFT: Long-duration mega-cap-tech I do not want as rates stay higher-for-longer and breadth is this thin. Quality business, wrong regime to lead with; reduce to zero.
discretionary-macro.NOW: High-multiple long-duration software, the most rate-sensitive corner of the crowded tech trade. Multiple compression risk as the long end stays bid-up; reduce to zero.
discretionary-macro.NTAP: Storage hardware riding the AI-build momentum; a tactical/technical name, not a macro-regime expression. Hold, no weight.
discretionary-macro.NVDA: Fade the crowded long-duration mega-cap-tech beta. In a regime where the Fed can't cut and the long end is bear-steepening (GS30 5.03), high-duration AI valuations face multiple compression; BofA flags a dangerously narrow tape (dot-com-2000 echo). Rearranging deck chairs in the wrong regime.
discretionary-macro.PFE: Large-cap pharma but a low-growth, patent-cliff story rather than the secular rate-insensitive compounder I want (LLY). Hold, no weight.
discretionary-macro.PM: Same late-cycle defensive thesis as MO with international/pricing-power cash flow; a real inflation pass-through name to hold while I keep gross exposure to growth low.
discretionary-macro.STX: Disk-drive cyclical leveraged to the AI-storage trade; bottom-up momentum, not my regime call. Hold at zero.
discretionary-macro.T: Same levered-telecom-into-a-steepener concern as VZ; not the clean defensive I want. Hold at zero.
discretionary-macro.TMUS: Better-positioned telecom but a growth-telecom multiple in a rate-pressured tape; not a regime expression for me. Hold, no weight.
discretionary-macro.UBER: Consumer-discretionary platform exposed to the sentiment collapse; not a defensive or inflation-winner name. Hold at zero.
discretionary-macro.UNH: Defensive healthcare cash flow with pricing power that passes cost-push inflation through. Late-cycle ballast that doesn't depend on a Fed pivot or discretionary consumer spend.
discretionary-macro.VZ: Defensive bond-proxy telecom but heavily levered into a bear-steepening long end (GS30 5.03), which pressures the debt-funded model. I prefer DUK as my regulated bond-proxy ballast. Hold, no weight.
discretionary-macro.WFC: Domestic-bank credit-cycle exposure into a rolling-over real economy; not a regime winner I want to own through the turn. Hold, no weight.
discretionary-macro.XOM: Primary trade: cleanest expression of a cost-push, energy-led stagflation regime. Brent ~$97-100 with a Strait-of-Hormuz supply disruption flows straight to integrated-major cash flow. Energy is the sector that historically wins when inflation re-accelerates (CPI 332.4, PCE 130.9) and the Fed can't cut (FEDFUNDS pinned 3.63).

growth.AMZN: AWS reaccelerated to +28% YoY (fastest in 15 quarters), >$15B AI run-rate, record 13.1% op margin from custom silicon. Three reinvestment vectors — retail, ads, cloud — under a 34x PE.
growth.APP: AI-driven ad engine compounding fast: 222% ROE, 88% gross margin, 30x PS. High beta (2.5) but the AXON machine-learning ad runway is real and lengthening — exactly the under-priced-runway profile I act on.
growth.BAC: Money-center bank — rate-sensitive, GDP-linked earnings, no secular growth runway. A cyclical-value name, not a compounder I pay up for.
growth.BMY: Patent-cliff pharma with declining base revenue and a low multiple reflecting structural decline — a value trap I avoid.
growth.C: Money-center bank turnaround story at low multiple — mean-reversion thesis, not durable compounding. Cheapness without a lengthening runway is not my signal.
growth.CHTR: Cable broadband losing subscribers to fiber/fixed-wireless — declining runway behind a cheap multiple. Value trap by my lens.
growth.CI: Managed care has steady earnings but regulatory and MLR headwinds cap the runway; no durable acceleration to justify growth capital.
growth.CL: Defensive consumer staple, low-single-digit organic growth — durable but no reinvestment runway. Quality without the compounding curve.
growth.CMCSA: Cheap-but-stagnant cable/media — declining broadband and video, no lengthening runway. A value trap by my lens; cheapness without growth is precisely what I screen out.
growth.COP: E&P shale producer levered to commodity price; capital-return story, not durable high growth. Not a compounder I pay up for.
growth.CVS: Integrated health/pharmacy under margin pressure with no growth acceleration; cheap because the runway is shrinking. Not a compounder.
growth.CVX: Same as XOM — mature integrated oil, dividend-and-buyback story, no lengthening growth runway to act on.
growth.DELL: AI-server backlog gives a cyclical lift, but commoditized hardware margins and a momentum-driven move, not a durable widening moat. I prefer the silicon (NVDA) over the box.
growth.DUK: Regulated utility, rate-base growth capped in the low single digits — the antithesis of a high-runway compounder.
growth.ELV: Managed care with MLR/regulatory headwinds capping the runway; no durable acceleration to justify growth capital this week.
growth.FTNT: Quality cybersecurity grower (155% ROE, 80% gross margin) but 57x PE with decelerating top line — runway is solid, not lengthening. Watch, but my conviction capital goes to the AI-infra and platform compounders.
growth.HCA: Steady hospital operator with leveraged balance sheet; durable cash flow but low-to-mid single-digit volume growth — outside my high-runway lens.
growth.HLT: Same as MAR — capital-light hotel franchising is a steady compounder but cyclical demand and a modest runway; not where my conviction capital goes.
growth.JPM: Best-in-class bank with strong ROE, but earnings are macro/rate-driven with a low-single-digit growth runway — quality, not the long compounding curve.
growth.KO: Defensive staple with low-single-digit volume growth — durable but not a long reinvestment runway. Quality, but outside the compounding curve I hunt.
growth.LLY: Durable secular compounder: GLP-1/obesity TAM barely penetrated, 101% ROE, 83% gross margin. 53x PE is the price of a multi-year volume runway the multiple does not fully capture; low beta adds quality.
growth.MAR: Asset-light lodging compounds modestly via fees, but travel is late-cycle and the runway is mid-single-digit — quality, not the long curve I size up.
growth.MAS: Building-products cyclical tied to housing — no secular runway, earnings mean-revert with the cycle. Not a compounder I act on.
growth.META: Accelerating, not maturing: revenue +33% YoY (fastest since 2021), net income +61%, AI ad targeting lifting price-per-ad +12% and impressions +19%. 24.9x PE prices deceleration into a re-accelerating compounder.
growth.MO: Declining-volume tobacco — high yield masking structural decline. The clearest anti-growth name in the set.
growth.MRK: Keytruda patent cliff looms with no offsetting runway acceleration; quality pharma but the compounding curve is flattening, not extending.
growth.MSFT: Broadest AI compounder: Azure AI + Copilot + enterprise software compound together at 30x PE, 33% ROE, 68% gross margin. Durable double-digit growth the multiple treats as ordinary.
growth.NOW: Purest software runway: subscription revenue +22%, cRPO +22.5%, $1M+ Now Assist customers +130% YoY. 66x PE is the price of the most-contracted agentic-AI backlog in enterprise software — I size on durability, not the multiple.
growth.NTAP: Storage benefits from AI data growth but is a mature, slow-grower riding a momentum trade — no lengthening runway to pay up for.
growth.NVDA: Spearhead AI-infra compounder: data-center revenue +92% YoY to $75.2B, Blackwell sold out through mid-2026, 111% ROE, 74% gross margin. 42x PE under-prices a multi-year reinvestment runway — supply, not demand, is the only ceiling.
growth.PFE: Post-COVID revenue cliff, declining base, pipeline gaps. Low multiple reflects structural decline — a value trap by my lens.
growth.PM: Tobacco with a smoke-free transition story, but the base business declines and the runway is defensive, not high-growth. Not my mandate.
growth.STX: HDD maker in a structurally challenged, cyclical category; momentum spike, not a compounder. Outside my mandate.
growth.T: No-growth, debt-heavy telecom; yield story tied to a saturated market. A static-value name I screen out.
growth.TMUS: Steady FCF and subscriber gains, but telecom is a low-runway, capital-heavy business at 17.5x PE — durable cash flow, not the long compounding curve my mandate hunts.
growth.UBER: Self-funding network-effects compounder: gross bookings +21% to $53.7B, AV trips up 10x, $9.8B FCF, Uber One at 50M. 14.6x PE barely prices the AV/advertising/membership runway.
growth.UNH: Large managed-care compounder historically, but current MLR/regulatory pressure caps near-term runway acceleration; no growth edge to commit capital this week.
growth.VZ: High-yield, no-growth telecom with heavy debt and saturated market. A static DCF name, not a compounder — outside my mandate.
growth.WFC: Post-asset-cap recovery is a mean-reversion story, not durable compounding. Outside my runway-first lens.
growth.XOM: Commodity-cyclical energy with no secular growth runway; earnings tied to oil price, not compounding reinvestment. Outside my mandate.

quant-systematic.AMZN: Growth/quality but low value score and modest ROE on trailing basis; momentum not standout. Below composite cut.
quant-systematic.APP: Extreme momentum but extreme vol and rich value; pure single-factor spike. Momentum sleeve already covered by confirmed names with better risk.
quant-systematic.BAC: Value (P/E 12.5) but ROE 10.5 modest and beta 1.20; quality+low-vol scores weak. Below composite cut among financials.
quant-systematic.BMY: Deep-value pharma (low P/E) but weak quality and momentum — low-quality value/value-trap profile; avoided by the joint-factor rule.
quant-systematic.C: Value (P/B 0.98) but low quality (ROE 7.6) and high beta 1.11; weak quality drags the composite below the financial peers and the shortlist.
quant-systematic.CHTR: Value cable like CMCSA but momentum weak/below trend — value-trap signature. Avoid the unconfirmed cheap name.
quant-systematic.CI: Value+low-beta managed-care (P/E 12.8, beta 0.29) but momentum neutral-to-weak; composite below cut. No marginal factor contribution.
quant-systematic.CL: Low-vol (beta 0.33) but P/E 32.5 is expensive and momentum neutral; the low-vol sleeve is cheaper and better in MO. Redundant and pricey.
quant-systematic.CMCSA: Extreme value (P/E 4.2) but RSI 31, below SMA200, negative MACD — classic value trap. Value and momentum disagree; avoid the unconfirmed cheap name.
quant-systematic.COP: Value energy, higher beta, momentum weak; cyclical single-sector bet that breadth does not support. Excluded.
quant-systematic.CVS: Deep value but weak quality and momentum; classic low-quality value name that the joint factor profile avoids.
quant-systematic.CVX: Value+dividend energy but neutral momentum and quality; redundant with XOM and below composite cut.
quant-systematic.DELL: Extreme-momentum tail: +61.9% 4w, ADX 56, deep above SMA200, but ATR ~7.7% of price (worst low-vol). Sized small for marginal momentum breadth, not concentration.
quant-systematic.DUK: Low-vol utility but value-neutral and no momentum edge; defensive sleeve already filled at better composite by MO.
quant-systematic.ELV: Value managed-care (P/E low) but momentum unconfirmed — value-trap risk; below composite cut, no factor edge.
quant-systematic.FTNT: Cleanest pure-trend factor: ADX 61 (strongest in set), RSI 72, price far above SMA200 (87). Software-momentum sleeve diversifying the hardware names.
quant-systematic.HCA: Deep value (P/E 12.2) but RSI 22.7, below SMA200, negative MACD — textbook value trap. Value and momentum disagree; avoid.
quant-systematic.HLT: Momentum+low-vol+quality stack mirroring MAR: RSI 62, MACD+, price>SMA200, ATR ~2.2%, ROE 171. Diversifies lodging cluster on the same favorable loadings.
quant-systematic.JPM: Highest-quality bank (ROE 16.3) but P/E 14.7 value-neutral and momentum not standout; solid composite but edged out by the momentum+low-vol leaders.
quant-systematic.KO: Low-vol staple but value-neutral and momentum modest; composite below MO/MAS for the same defensive factor exposure.
quant-systematic.LLY: High quality+momentum pharma but extremely rich value and high vol; single-story crowded name. Breadth book passes.
quant-systematic.MAR: Balanced momentum+quality+low-vol: +10.9% 4w, RSI 68, price>SMA200, top-decile ROE, ATR ~2.2% of price. Rich P/E 40 is the one weak factor, offset by quality+low-vol.
quant-systematic.MAS: Value+quality+low-vol with neutral momentum: P/E 17.3, ROE 280, RSI 52. Low correlation to the momentum leg — diversifying value factor exposure.
quant-systematic.META: Quality+momentum but rich value and narrative-driven crowding; composite competitive but below the cleaner momentum+low-vol leaders chosen for breadth.
quant-systematic.MO: Best low-vol/value/quality anchor: lowest ATR/price (~2.1%), beta 0.51, P/E 17.4 value tilt, ROE 150, price>SMA200. Breadth ballast against the momentum sleeve.
quant-systematic.MRK: Value+quality pharma but momentum unconfirmed; composite below the shortlist cut. No factor edge over the chosen names.
quant-systematic.MSFT: High quality, value-neutral, moderate momentum; solid but no top-decile factor and crowded. Excluded to keep breadth across factors rather than mega-cap tilt.
quant-systematic.NOW: High-multiple growth, weak value factor, high vol; momentum present but the cleaner software-trend exposure is in FTNT. Excluded.
quant-systematic.NTAP: Top composite: momentum +45.5% 4w, ADX 56, OBV positive (volume-confirmed), RSI 72, price >> SMA200; quality ROE high, P/E ~26 value-neutral. Strongest confirmed momentum+quality leg.
quant-systematic.NVDA: Strong momentum+quality but very rich on value and high realized vol; a single-story crowded name. My breadth book caps mega-cap concentration in favor of diversified factor loadings.
quant-systematic.PFE: Deep-value pharma with weak/negative momentum — value-trap signature where cheapness and trend disagree; mandate trusts the joint profile and avoids.
quant-systematic.PM: Low-vol/quality (beta 0.42, ROE high) but P/E 24.5 is value-neutral and MO already fills the low-vol/value sleeve at a cheaper multiple. Redundant exposure.
quant-systematic.STX: Storage-momentum peer of NTAP/DELL; momentum present but cluster already covered. Adding would concentrate the same factor — excluded for breadth.
quant-systematic.T: Cheapest on P/E 7.2 but RSI 24, below SMA200, negative OBV — deepest value-trap signature in the set. Joint factor profile says no.
quant-systematic.TMUS: Quality telecom but value-neutral and momentum modest; composite below the chosen low-vol/momentum names. No marginal contribution.
quant-systematic.UBER: Momentum-only story name; value and quality factors weak, vol high. No balanced composite — fails the breadth test.
quant-systematic.UNH: Quality managed-care but momentum weak this window and value-neutral; composite below cut.
quant-systematic.VZ: Value (P/E 11) + very low beta 0.24, but trend weak and momentum unconfirmed. Low-vol sleeve better filled by MO at a comparable multiple with positive trend.
quant-systematic.WFC: Balanced value+quality (P/E 11.8, ROE 12) and beta 0.93, but momentum unconfirmed; best financial on composite yet still below the shortlist names.
quant-systematic.XOM: Value+quality energy but momentum unconfirmed and sector cyclical; composite below cut, no marginal factor edge over the shortlist.

risk-officer.AMZN: High-beta mega-cap with consumer-discretionary sensitivity at sentiment lows (UMich 49.8). Correlated to the growth cluster — no diversification value here.
risk-officer.APP: Highest-beta, most-speculative momentum name in the set; severe drawdown history and thin downside support in a vol-regime break. The clearest tail to avoid entirely.
risk-officer.BAC: Bank with rate/credit-cycle fragility into a bear-steepening, weak-sentiment regime; correlated to C/WFC/JPM. Cyclical tail not ballast — zero weight.
risk-officer.BMY: Low-vol pharma diversifier, low beta and lowly correlated to the IT/growth cluster. Patent-cliff is the caveat — small size as marginal defensive ballast.
risk-officer.C: Bank balance-sheet fragility into a bear-steepening, weak-sentiment tape; correlated to BAC/WFC/JPM. Adds cyclical tail, not ballast. Zero weight.
risk-officer.CHTR: Levered cable balance sheet with secular-decline and subscriber tail; more fragile than the dividend telecoms. No ballast value, zero weight.
risk-officer.CI: Managed-care names carry policy/MLR tail and cluster with UNH/ELV/HCA; no diversification benefit. Stay flat until the healthcare drawdown path is clearer.
risk-officer.CL: Defensive staples ballast: ADX 20, low ATR, low correlation to the crowded growth cluster. Counterweight whose value is whole-portfolio drawdown reduction in a vol spike.
risk-officer.CMCSA: Low-beta cable/telecom, modest vol — a tolerable diversifier away from the AI-momentum cluster, but secular-decline tail caps conviction. Small size only.
risk-officer.COP: E&P is higher-beta to oil than the integrateds and correlates with XOM/CVX. Energy exposure already covered; no marginal diversification, zero weight.
risk-officer.CVS: Healthcare cluster with managed-care MLR and integration tail; correlates with the UNH/CI/ELV downside path. Avoid the continuation drawdown; flat.
risk-officer.CVX: Integrated-energy ballast, low correlation to growth, conservative balance sheet. Diversifying inflation hedge — but cluster-cap energy with XOM/COP so the bet isn't double-counted.
risk-officer.DELL: Most stretched name in the screen: RSI 69, ADX 56, ATR ~6.5% of price, +62%/4w, ~2.5x its 200d SMA. Anchor of the correlated AI-hardware cluster — extreme momentum-unwind tail. Zero weight.
risk-officer.DUK: Regulated-utility ballast: low beta, neutral RSI 43, stable cash flows. Rate-sensitivity is the only caveat — size modest, valued for correlation reduction.
risk-officer.ELV: Managed-care policy/MLR tail, part of the falling-knife healthcare cluster. Correlated downside, no diversification — zero weight.
risk-officer.FTNT: Strongest trend in the screen (RSI 72, ADX 61) and most overbought security-software name. A vol/momentum break draws it down with the IT cluster — correlated tail, zero weight.
risk-officer.HCA: Falling-knife caution: RSI 22, below 50/200d SMAs, -16% momentum. Looks cheap but carries continuation-drawdown risk; do not average into the trend. Flat until it stabilizes.
risk-officer.HLT: Lodging cyclical correlated with MAR; consumer-sensitive into weak sentiment. Adds cyclical tail, not diversification. Zero weight.
risk-officer.JPM: Best-capitalized, most-survivable bank — if any bank exposure is warranted it is this one. But banks correlate as a bloc into the cycle; only a token weight, cluster-capped.
risk-officer.KO: Best low-vol staples ballast: ATR ~$1.5, ADX 22, neutral RSI 50, low beta to the AI-momentum complex. Lowers whole-portfolio drawdown in a VaR-stress scenario.
risk-officer.LLY: Quality pharma but trades like a momentum growth name with a stretched multiple — more beta than ballast. Pharma defense already taken via MRK; flat here.
risk-officer.MAR: Consumer-cyclical lodging into a sentiment trough (UMich 49.8); correlates with HLT and the discretionary growth trade. Cyclical tail, no ballast value — flat.
risk-officer.MAS: Housing-linked industrial, cyclical and rate-sensitive into a weak-consumer tape. No defensive role; zero weight.
risk-officer.META: Mega-cap growth, high beta, correlated to the AI complex. Standalone story is fine but it concentrates an already-crowded exposure; zero weight on risk grounds.
risk-officer.MO: Genuine low-vol ballast: ATR ~$1.5, ADX 18, neutral RSI 53, low beta to the IT momentum complex. Diversifying defensive exposure that reduces whole-book variance.
risk-officer.MRK: Defensive large-cap pharma: low beta, stable cash flows, low correlation to the IT-momentum cluster. Diversifying ballast that lowers portfolio variance in a vol spike.
risk-officer.MSFT: Highest-quality mega-cap and the least-fragile balance sheet in the AI complex, but still beta-heavy and crowded. Minimal token weight only, not a core hold.
risk-officer.NOW: High-multiple software, high beta, multiple-compression tail in a rising-vol regime. Concentrates the growth factor — zero weight.
risk-officer.NTAP: Direct DELL peer (RSI 72, +46%/4w). Holding both double-counts one momentum factor bet — exactly the hidden correlation that turns a single-name loss into a portfolio drawdown. Zero.
risk-officer.NVDA: Anchor of the crowded AI-infrastructure trade; high beta into a VIX-regime break (21.51). Concentrated factor tail the panel will pile into — I refuse to add to it.
risk-officer.PFE: Defensive pharma with low vol, but post-COVID earnings erosion is a continuation-drawdown caution. Tiny size as a marginal diversifier only.
risk-officer.PM: Low-vol staples diversifier (ADX 22) lowly correlated to the AI-momentum cluster. Ballast that earns inclusion for portfolio-risk reduction, not standalone return; FX is the caveat — sized small.
risk-officer.STX: AI-storage cluster peer of DELL/NTAP; same crowded momentum factor, same regime-shift tail. No incremental diversification — avoid.
risk-officer.T: Low-beta telecom defensive, high dividend, lowly correlated to growth/IT. Diversifying ballast — cluster-cap with VZ so telecom isn't over-weighted as one bet.
risk-officer.TMUS: Lower-beta wireless with better balance sheet than the wireline telecoms; modest defensive value. Telecom cluster already covered by VZ/T — keep size minimal.
risk-officer.UBER: High-beta consumer-platform name exposed to a weakening consumer; adds cyclical/growth tail without diversification. Avoid.
risk-officer.UNH: Managed-care policy/MLR tail and a falling-knife pattern across the cluster (CI/ELV/HCA). Continuation-drawdown risk outweighs defensive label; zero weight.
risk-officer.VZ: Genuine low-vol defensive ballast: high dividend, low beta, lowly correlated to the crowded growth/IT complex. Earns a place for whole-book drawdown reduction, not upside.
risk-officer.WFC: Same bank-cluster credit/rate tail as BAC/C. No marginal diversification; adds correlated cyclical exposure. Flat.
risk-officer.XOM: Energy diversifier: low correlation to the IT/growth cluster and a partial stagflation hedge given long-end stress. Strong balance sheet; ballast role, sized small.

technical.AMZN: Weakest momentum of the mega-caps: RSI 37, MACD hist -3.69, price rolling off EMA20. Above SMA200 but the near-term trend has turned down — stand aside until support holds.
technical.APP: Right at its SMA200 541 with a choppy ADX 30 and RSI 60 — a base, not a confirmed trend. Watch for a clean reclaim of the long-term average before positioning.
technical.BAC: Just above a flat SMA200 52 with RSI 62 and turning MACD — recovering but range-bound (ADX 16), not yet a confirmed trend. C is the cleaner financial; hold off here.
technical.BMY: Pharma not in my leadership screen; no confirmed uptrend on the tape. A cheap-and-flat name is not a buy to me — stand aside.
technical.C: Clean uptrend: price 128 above stacked EMA20 128 > EMA50 125 > SMA200 111, RSI 63, MACD positive and rising (hist +0.90), ADX 21 building. Strong relative strength among the financials, under accumulation (rising OBV).
technical.CHTR: Cable name, not a leader on the tape; no confirmed trend in my read. No position.
technical.CI: Managed-care name absent from my leadership screen; UNH is the cleaner relative-strength read in the group. No charted setup here, so no position.
technical.CL: Consumer-staples defensive absent from my leadership screen; no confirmed uptrend on the tape. Stand aside — a flat staple is not a setup.
technical.CMCSA: Not in the leadership/momentum screen; cable name with no confirmed trend on my read. Absence from the relative-strength shortlist plus no charted breakout means stand aside, no position.
technical.COP: Flat and trendless (ADX 11), price sitting on its EMAs, RSI 49. Constructive longer-term but no setup to act on now.
technical.CVS: Healthcare-retail name absent from leadership; no charted uptrend. Stand aside — UNH/LLY carry the sector's relative strength.
technical.CVX: Rangebound: ADX 12, price at EMA20/EMA50, MACD barely turning. Above SMA200 but no confirmed trend — no edge on the tape, no position.
technical.DELL: Cleanest setup on the board: textbook stacked trend (price 400 > EMA20 338 > EMA50 268 > SMA200 163), ADX 56, and a gap from ~317 to ~421 on a volume blowout then a tight 390-435 flag on contracting volume — accumulation. 386-394 shelf is support.
technical.DUK: Below EMA50 125 and right at SMA200; RSI 43, MACD negative. No uptrend to position with — utility is consolidating, not breaking out.
technical.ELV: Managed-care name not in my momentum screen; no confirmed setup. UNH is the cleaner relative-strength read. No position.
technical.FTNT: Strongest trend reading in the screen (ADX 61), fresh 52-week high, perfectly stacked MAs, rising OBV. Flattening MACD hist near a vertical move says add on confirmation, not chase — but the leadership is undeniable.
technical.HCA: Per my screen, sits below a declining SMA200 with ADX under 20 and negative OBV — distribution and no trend. Not a buy regardless of the fundamental story.
technical.HLT: Constructive uptrend: price 330 above EMA20 330 > EMA50 322 > SMA200 294, RSI 62, MACD positive with rising histogram. ADX 18 is modest so a smaller position, but the structure confirms the same lodging strength as MAR.
technical.JPM: Right at its SMA200 306 with RSI 59 and MACD just crossing up; constructive but ADX 20 and price below the long-term average mean no confirmed trend yet. C leads the group.
technical.KO: Mild uptrend above SMA200 but ADX 22 with a rolling MACD hist; RSI 51, OBV negative. Quiet, not a leader — stand aside for cleaner setups.
technical.LLY: Premier uptrend on the board: price 1064 above stacked EMA20 1064 > EMA50 1014 > SMA200 950, RSI 71, MACD strongly positive (hist +6.6), ADX 33. Powerful confirmed trend under accumulation — lean in.
technical.MAR: Clean breakout to a 52-week high, price above stacked EMAs 374/361 over SMA200 314, RSI 68, MACD turning up. Modest ADX 25 means early-stage trend — ride it with a tight stop under the breakout shelf.
technical.MAS: Flat: ADX 13, price hugging its EMAs and just above SMA200, MACD hist ~0, OBV slightly negative. No trend to position with — stand aside.
technical.META: Distribution: price below EMA20/EMA50 and well under SMA200 661, RSI 40, MACD negative, OBV negative. Broken structure, not a buy regardless of the story.
technical.MO: Quiet, steady uptrend: price above stacked EMA20 71 > EMA50 70 > SMA200 64, RSI 53, positive OBV. ADX 18 keeps it small, but it is a defensive name actually trending up rather than rolling over.
technical.MRK: Pharma defensive, not in my momentum screen; no confirmed uptrend. LLY is the relative-strength leader in healthcare, not MRK. Stand aside.
technical.MSFT: Below SMA200 (456) with RSI 45 and negative MACD hist; price stuck at EMA20. The leadership name is in a corrective phase, not a confirmed uptrend. No position.
technical.NOW: Best momentum of the software mega-caps in my read: RSI 56, MACD positive with rising histogram, price above EMA20/EMA50, ADX 28. Below its SMA200 still, so a small early-trend position, not a core add.
technical.NTAP: Mirrors DELL's structure: stacked EMAs 150/129 over SMA200 113, ADX 56, expanding positive MACD. RSI 72 is extended so size it as a buy-the-dip leader, not a chase.
technical.NVDA: AI leader in pullback: RSI 47, MACD hist negative (-2.15), ADX collapsed to 19, price below EMA20 and bumping the BB middle. Trend has stalled; the chart does not confirm the growth narrative. No position until it reclaims momentum.
technical.PFE: No leadership signature on the tape; a cheap-and-flat pharma is not a buy to me. Absent from the screen, no charted setup, no position.
technical.PM: Rolling over: RSI 47, MACD hist negative (-1.63), price pulled back below recent highs though still above SMA200. Momentum has turned down — stand aside until it stabilizes.
technical.STX: Major uptrend (ADX 44, price 880 far above stacked EMAs 836/711 and SMA200 390) in an orderly pullback — negative MACD hist and price off the upper Bollinger band read as mean-reversion toward the EMA20 within a strong trend, a constructive entry.
technical.T: Telecom not in my momentum screen; no charted breakout. Stand aside until relative strength appears.
technical.TMUS: Clear downtrend: price below EMA20 187 < EMA50 193 < SMA200 210, RSI 34, MACD negative, OBV negative. Broken structure — do not fight the tape.
technical.UBER: No trend (ADX 10) and below all moving averages including SMA200 83; RSI 41, negative OBV. Choppy and directionless — exactly where I stand aside.
technical.UNH: Strong confirmed trend: ADX 38 (very high), price above EMA20 383 > EMA50 360 > SMA200 329, RSI 70. Flattening MACD hist near a strong run argues respect the extension, but the relative-strength leadership earns a core position.
technical.VZ: Telecom defensive, not a momentum leader in my screen; no confirmed uptrend structure. Stand aside until the chart shows a reclaim of long-term averages.
technical.WFC: Below EMA50 79 and SMA200 84 despite RSI 62; price still under the long-term average. A bounce within a basing structure, not a confirmed uptrend — stand aside.
technical.XOM: Above SMA200 but flat: ADX 11, MACD hist ~0, price pinned at EMA20=EMA50. No trend to position with; rangebound energy is a stand-aside.

value.AMZN: Wonderful business, but earnings are reinvested into a story the multiple already capitalizes. No conservative discount to act on.
value.APP: Pure momentum/story stock at a valuation I cannot anchor to conservative cash flows. The discipline exists precisely to avoid this. Zero weight.
value.BAC: 12.5x P/E, 1.32x P/B, but ROE only 10.5% and beta 1.20. Citi's sub-book discount is a wider margin of safety on the same re-rating thesis — I'd rather own C in this cluster.
value.BMY: Pharma discount near its 52-week low: 16.6x P/E, 41.6% ROE, 53.7% gross margin, beta 0.23. D/E 2.44 is the watch item, so a smaller position, but normalized earnings power on a durable franchise clears a thinner margin-of-safety bar.
value.C: Sub-book financial at 0.98x P/B (below tangible book), 15.9x P/E. The discount to book IS the margin of safety; normalizing ROE plus buybacks shrinking the count below book is the re-rating path.
value.CHTR: Cable peer to CMCSA, but CMCSA is my cleaner, more diversified discount-to-FCF; no need to double the cable bet, and CHTR carries heavier leverage.
value.CI: Cheap quality: 12.8x P/E, 8.9x EV/EBITDA, 15% ROE, beta 0.29 — a high-return managed-care franchise priced like a no-growth utility. The discount on a durable business is the margin of safety.
value.CL: Quality staples compounder but consistently a premium multiple — no discount to intrinsic value on offer. Not a value entry.
value.CMCSA: Top conviction. ~4.2x P/E, ~4.8x EV/EBITDA, 0.68x P/S, ROE ~20%, 70% gross margin near the $23 52-week low; $3.9B Q1-2026 FCF with broadband losses improving YoY. The market is pricing terminal decline into a cash machine — the gap to conservative intrinsic value is the widest in the set. Watch item is ARPU/EBITDA deceleration, so this is a discount-to-cash-flow call, not a growth call.
value.COP: E&P earnings are a leveraged bet on the crude price, which I will not underwrite conservatively. 'Too hard' for a margin-of-safety call.
value.CVS: P/E distorted to 69x by impaired earnings, EV/EBITDA 27, ROE only 3.9% — the P/B 1.34 is not a clean discount. Cannot value conservatively; pass per Principle 3.
value.CVX: Same as XOM — energy here is a macro regime bet on the oil price, not a defensible per-share valuation gap. Pass on the commodity exposure.
value.DELL: AI-server momentum name with thin margins and a leveraged balance sheet; the price already pays for the growth story. Not a value entry.
value.DUK: Regulated-utility steadiness at a fair-to-full multiple with utility leverage; no conservative discount to intrinsic value to act on.
value.ELV: Managed-care peer to CI but I already own the cleaner discount in CI; no incremental margin of safety here to justify a second position in the cluster.
value.FTNT: Quality security franchise but a rich growth multiple — exactly the TAM/growth excitement the current price more than pays for. Pass on principle.
value.HCA: Strong operator but a momentum-favored name carrying real leverage; no defensible discount to intrinsic value at the current multiple.
value.HLT: Same as MAR — quality lodging at a premium multiple, no discount to intrinsic value to underwrite.
value.JPM: Best-quality bank at 16.3% ROE, but 2.42x P/B and 14.7x P/E is a premium for quality, not a discount. A wonderful business at a full price is not a value buy.
value.KO: Durable franchise but priced at a quality premium, not a margin of safety. I do not pay up for staples stability.
value.LLY: Exceptional franchise but priced for GLP-1 perfection — a story-and-growth multiple the value lens exists to avoid. A wonderful business at a punishing price is a bad investment.
value.MAR: Asset-light compounder but a full multiple and a momentum-crowd favorite this week; no valuation gap. Momentum is the noise my discipline filters.
value.MAS: Decent building-products business but fairly priced into a cyclical; no conservative discount on offer. Pass.
value.META: Strong cash generation but a growth-priced multiple with heavy AI capex; the gap to conservative value is not there at this price.
value.MO: 17.4x P/E, 12.7x EV/EBITDA, but D/E 10.4, current ratio 0.65 and negative book (P/B 27). The earnings yield is real but the balance sheet leaves no margin of safety on a secularly-declining volume base.
value.MRK: Quality pharma sold to its 52-week low ($76 vs $125) at 16.3x P/E, 18% ROE, 78% gross margin. The discount is on post-Keytruda-cliff earnings, not hard assets — a thinner-but-real margin on a durable franchise.
value.MSFT: Superb franchise at a premium multiple; quality earns a thinner margin, never a premium. No discount to intrinsic value here.
value.NOW: Premier SaaS compounder at an extreme growth multiple — the canonical 'paying up for the story' I avoid. Cannot value conservatively; zero weight.
value.NTAP: Storage momentum favorite this week — a technical/factor trade, not a defensible discount to intrinsic value. The chart is not my thesis.
value.NVDA: Extraordinary business, but the price embeds years of flawless AI-capex compounding — a story multiple with no margin of safety. I expect to look wrong in the rally and be vindicated in the drawdown.
value.PFE: Cheap pharma at 19x trailing P/E (depressed post-COVID earnings), 74.8% gross margin, covered dividend, 1.16 current ratio. Normalized earnings power and balance-sheet liquidity give downside protection at this price.
value.PM: 24.5x P/E and D/E 81 with a near-zero equity base — a quality-narrative name at a full multiple, not a value discount. Pass.
value.STX: Cyclical storage hardware riding momentum; earnings are cycle-dependent and the price reflects the upswing. No margin of safety.
value.T: Deepest-value telecom in the set: 7.2x P/E, 6.2x EV/EBITDA, 1.24x P/S, 19.7% ROE, beta 0.22, D/E only 1.23 (lighter than VZ). A discounted cash-flow stream with cleaner leverage than its peer — the balance sheet supports the margin of safety.
value.TMUS: Best-operator telecom but a growth-premium multiple — I get the discounted cash flow far cheaper in T and VZ. No margin of safety here.
value.UBER: Now profitable but priced for continued hyper-growth; normalized owner earnings do not support the multiple with any margin of safety. Pass.
value.UNH: Highest-quality managed care but I get the cluster exposure more cheaply through CI at 12.8x; UNH's multiple is not a discount today.
value.VZ: Defensive, low-volatility cash stream at 11x P/E, 7.5x EV/EBITDA, 16.7% ROE, beta 0.24. Discounted covered dividend; D/E 1.5 is telecom-normal infrastructure leverage, the only caveat. A sound business at a defensible discount.
value.WFC: 11.8x P/E, 12% ROE but 1.62x P/B — fairly priced, not discounted. No margin of safety versus C's below-tangible-book setup.
value.XOM: Quality integrated major but a top-down commodity-regime call, not a clean discount to conservative intrinsic value; earnings swing with a price I cannot forecast. Not my edge.
